retrieved_note: Re-verified 2026-05-25 against Jan-Jun 2026 KFS (current/saving), Jan-Jun 2025 SOC, and forthcoming Jul-Dec 2026 SOC. Pilot fees reflect the Jan-Jun 2026 KFS, which is the schedule in force as of the verification date.

notes: Jan-Jun 2026 current-account and saving-account KFS list 'Classic Union Pay Int.' annual fee at PKR 2,500.

Joining/issuance fee is free per Jul-Dec 2026 SOC section 6.i.a ("Issuance of Debit Card: Free"); the same free-issuance convention is implied across all Al Baraka debit cards in the Jan-Jun 2026 KFS.
Bank product page still markets this card as 'Al Baraka UnionPay Silver Debit Card'; the KFS uses 'Classic Union Pay Int.' for the matching fee row.
No public salary, balance, or age threshold tied specifically to the UnionPay Classic card; eligibility flows from the linked PKR current/saving account.
Forthcoming Jul-Dec 2026 SOC raises this annual fee to PKR 2,800.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: Free for Tier-2 customers who maintain a Monthly Average Balance of PKR 100,000 for 3 consecutive months (per Al Baraka SOC).

notes: Jan-Jun 2026 current-account and saving-account KFS list 'Gold Union Pay Int.' annual fee at PKR 3,500.
Gold-tier waiver: Jan-Jun 2025 SOC and Jul-Dec 2026 SOC both state a Tier-1 customer can upgrade to Tier-2 by maintaining MAB of PKR 100K for 3 months, after which the Gold Debit Card fee is waived.
Threshold recorded under minimum_average_balance_pkr (the bank's own term is Monthly Average Balance), not account-opening balance.
Joining fee free per Jul-Dec 2026 SOC "Issuance of Debit Card: Free".
Forthcoming Jul-Dec 2026 SOC raises annual fee to PKR 4,000.
Jan-Jun 2026 KFS rows list 'Platinum MasterCard' annual fee at PKR 10,000.

Corrected balance and waiver: pilot previously inherited a PKR 100,000 / 3-month rule from the Gold-tier SOC clause. The Jan-Jun 2025 and Jul-Dec 2026 SOC tier-upgrade text explicitly references the Gold Debit Card waiver only, not Platinum. No public Platinum-specific balance threshold was found; both fields reset to null.
Joining fee free per Jul-Dec 2026 SOC "Issuance of Debit Card: Free".
Forthcoming Jul-Dec 2026 SOC raises this annual fee to PKR 12,000.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: First-year fee waived for Shafqaat, Banaat, Consumer Business Partner, and Business Plus account holders maintaining the published MAB threshold (per Jan-Jun 2025 / Jul-Dec 2026 SOC).

notes: Jan-Jun 2026 KFS rows list 'Paypak Standard' annual fee at PKR 2,200.

Waiver: Jul-Dec 2026 SOC lists 'Free ATM/Debit Card (Paypak) for the first year' as a benefit of Shafqaat (MAB 10K), Banaat (MAB 10K), Consumer Business Partner (MAB 25K), and Business Plus Tier-1 (MAB 25K).
Joining fee free per Jul-Dec 2026 SOC "Issuance of Debit Card: Free".
Forthcoming Jul-Dec 2026 SOC raises this annual fee to PKR 2,500.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: Free for Tier-2 customers who maintain a Monthly Average Balance of PKR 100,000 for 3 consecutive months (Al Baraka SOC Gold Debit Card waiver).

notes: Jan-Jun 2026 current-account and saving-account KFS list 'Gold MasterCard' annual fee at PKR 4,500.
Gold-tier waiver: SOC explicitly states the Tier-2 (PKR 100K MAB sustained 3 months) free Gold Debit Card. Recorded under minimum_average_balance_pkr rather than minimum_account_balance_pkr.
Joining fee free per Jul-Dec 2026 SOC "Issuance of Debit Card: Free".
Forthcoming Jul-Dec 2026 SOC raises annual fee to PKR 5,000.

requirements.annual_fee_waiver_rule: Free for Freelancer (ESFCA) account customers maintaining MAB of PKR 10,000 and above; free for Plus-tier Payroll Account holders (per Jan-Jun 2026 Payroll KFS).

notes: Jan-Jun 2026 saving-account and current-account KFS list 'Classic MasterCard' annual fee at PKR 3,200; the product page markets the same tier as 'Al Baraka Silver debit Mastercard'.

Corrected: previous pilot listed minimum_account_balance_pkr=10000 sourced to older material; the current Jan-Jun 2026 KFS does not restate any Silver-specific balance threshold, so balance is reset to null (per verification methodology silence != 0).

Waivers identified: Freelancer Account footnote ("Free for Freelancer Account customers on opening or maintaining monthly average balance of 10k and above"); Jan-Jun 2026 Payroll KFS shows Plus tier gets Classic MasterCard FREE.
Joining fee free per Jul-Dec 2026 SOC "Issuance of Debit Card: Free".
Forthcoming Jul-Dec 2026 SOC raises annual fee to PKR 3,500.

Jan-Jun 2026 KFS rows list 'Classic Union Pay Int.' at PKR 2,500. The bank's UnionPay debit-card product page markets two tiers ('Al Baraka UnionPay Silver Debit Card' and 'Al Baraka UnionPay Gold Debit Card'); the KFS fee table calls the entry-tier 'Classic'. The pilot keeps both 'Classic' and 'Silver' entries because the product page uses Silver naming.
Corrected: salary and balance reset from 0 to null per methodology (silence is not zero).
Joining fee free per Jul-Dec 2026 SOC "Issuance of Debit Card: Free".
Forthcoming Jul-Dec 2026 SOC raises annual fee to PKR 2,800.
Confidence medium because UnionPay Silver and UnionPay Classic may in fact be the same product under two marketing names; the bank does not publish a separate fee row for Silver.
Jan-Jun 2026 KFS list 'Paypak NexGen' annual fee at PKR 2,200; Jul-Dec 2026 SOC re-labels it 'NexGen Banking PayPak Debit Card' at PKR 2,500.

Eligibility inherited from the Al Baraka Tifl-Young Savers Account, the bank's underage-saver product ("can be opened for any child and teenager aged between one day and 18 years"). Linked-account opening balance PKR 100; saving KFS confirms Tifl 'To open PKR 100' and 'To keep NIL'.
Min age recorded as 0 (Tifl explicitly accepts one-day-old; parent/guardian operated). Max age 18 per the Tifl page.
Joining fee free per Jul-Dec 2026 SOC "Issuance of Debit Card: Free".

Confidence medium because Al Baraka does not publish a card-specific eligibility KFS; the Tifl linkage is inferred from the NexGen brand (a minors-focused product) and from the segment match between the Tifl account and the NexGen card.

gaps: No public consumer credit-card catalogue was found on the Al Baraka domain in this verification pass; Al Baraka Pakistan only issues debit cards.
Jan-Jun 2026 KFS additionally lists a 'Paypak Aura' debit card (PKR 3,000) and a women-focused 'Aura Card' product page — not currently in the pilot set.

Bank product pages do not publish per-card salary, age, or absolute balance thresholds; all derived thresholds come from linked-account inheritance or the SOC's Tier-2 / Freelancer / Shafqaat / Banaat / CBP / Payroll waiver footnotes.

Annual fees change every six months in the SOC cycle; Jul-Dec 2026 SOC raises every debit card variant. Current pilot values reflect the Jan-Jun 2026 KFS, which is the schedule in force on the 2026-05-25 verification date.
